Familiar Bluet

  1. Home
  3. Gallery
  4. Animals
  5. Insects
  6. Damselflies
  7. Familiar Bluet

Blue-form Female Familiar Bluet

Familiar Bluet in Sunlight

Warm-toned Female Familiar Bluet

Enallagma civile

A slender Familiar Bluet rests among broad leaves and marsh-edge vegetation, its narrow body and folded wings blending easily into the green margins of the wetland. In bright sun the wing lattice becomes visible and the abdomen shows alternating pale and darker markings, while shaded views emphasize the insect’s delicacy and stillness more than its color.

This species is commonly encountered around ponds, marshes, and slow-moving water, where it perches quietly on grasses, sedges, and leaf tips between short, fluttering flights. Individuals are often abundant in open, sunlit habitats and are among the most frequently observed damselflies in midsummer.

For identification details and comparison with similar species, see Enallagma civile in the Field Notes section.

Blue-form Female Familiar Bluet
A blue-form female Familiar Bluet rests on a leaf, showing the slender body, folded wings, and alternating blue-and-dark abdominal markings typical of bluets.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mk IV • Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 200 • Aperture f/13 • Shutter 1/125
E21A1640 • Size: 1849x2773

Familiar Bluet in Sunlight
A blue-form female Familiar Bluet rests along the edge of a leaf, with strong sunlight revealing the fine lattice of the wings and casting a crisp shadow below.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mk IV • Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 200 • Aperture f/13 • Shutter 1/125
E21A1516 • Size: 2048x1365

Warm-toned Female Familiar Bluet
A female Familiar Bluet with warmer coloration rests on a broad leaf, showing the variability that can make bluet identification challenging from photographs alone.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mk IV • Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 200 • Aperture f/13 • Shutter 1/125
E21A1646 • Size: 2048x1365

Familiar Bluet Resting on a Leaf
A Familiar Bluet rests above marsh vegetation, holding its clear wings folded over the abdomen while perched quietly between short flights.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mk IV • Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 200 • Aperture f/13 • Shutter 1/125
E21A1519 • Size: 2048x1365
